content.full_text:
July 20, 1992

Violet Hernbrow
P.O. Box 9105
South Lake Tahoe, Ca. 96158-2005

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, Ca. 95804-0807

RE: Request for guidance information re political statements in newsletters
of non-profit corporations

There is a dispute within our homeowner's association regarding the
propriety of a candidate making a political statement under the guise of
the President's message of the homeowners association. A copy of the
newsletter is attached.

Either Mr. or Mrs Robert Bonadio read the total statement on the phone to
Mrs. Cassidy of your office in June. It is the understanding of the
Bonadio's that Mrs. Cassidy's opinion was that the Homeowner's
Association had become an unwilling political action committee because of
the use of the word "support." They also understood that civil damages
could be recovered by the Homeowner's Association.

The candidate and association president, Mr. Brannon Smith, states that
he checked with your office prior to publication and was assured that a
statement did not violate the guidelines of the FPCC.

Through my membership in other organizations, I understand that political
messages jeopardize the tax exempt status of an organization. I understand
that this question is probably one for a different department of
government, but I would appreciate any information you can provide.

Our homeowner's association will be formulating guidelines for the future
as well as determining any past error at a future meeting. Therefore, I
would appreciate any information you can provide to help us make a
reasonable rule that complies with the law.

Respectfully,

Violet Hernbrow
(Mrs. Frank Hernbrow)

CC: Mr. & Mrs. Robert Bonadio

sections.question: There is a dispute within our homeowners' association regarding the propriety of a candidate making a political statement under the guise of the President's message of the homeowners association newsletter. The candidate/association president states he checked with the FPPC office prior to publication and was assured the statement did not violate FPPC guidelines, while others understand a prior FPPC staff opinion indicated the association had become an unwilling political action committee because of the use of the word 'support.' The requestor seeks guidance on whether such political messages in homeowners association newsletters violate FPPC guidelines and how they affect the association's tax-exempt status.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a political statement made by a candidate/homeowners association president in the association's newsletter violate FPPC guidelines, and what are the implications for the organization's legal status?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a homeowners association member, not an FPPC response. Document contains conflicting accounts of prior FPPC staff guidance. No formal question/conclusion sections present. References an attached newsletter statement but attachment not included in text provided. Minor OCR errors present (e.g., 'homeorazner's' for 'homeowners').

The requestor seeks guidance on whether such political messages in homeowners association newsletters violate FPPC guidelines and how they affect the association's tax-exempt status.

embedding.summary: Violet Hembrow requests FPPC guidance regarding a dispute within her homeowners association about whether the association president/candidate's political statement in the association newsletter violates FPPC guidelines and jeopardizes the organization's tax-exempt status.
